scope: Actual LightningIndexer compressed-dKV diagnostic that keeps dot-K selected-key batching and replaces global per-selected atomics with sparse local active-list accumulation plus coalesced global writes.
read: dot_k_sparse tests the missing middle between dense-route dot_k and sparse-atomic dot_k_atomic. It keeps selected-key batched tl.dot for contrib[K,D], avoids the dense route[active_group,K] matrix, and accumulates one row per active compressed group before global writes. It proves the coalesced write count is executable at S128/S512, but the local sparse accumulation is scalar-shaped and becomes slower than all retained alternatives by S512. Therefore the next production path cannot be simple per-selected local active-list scatter in Triton; it needs a true segmented/MFMA-friendly streaming reduction or LI/compressor fusion before dKV writes.

decision.why: The kernel achieves active-list coalesced writes, but local sparse accumulation costs dominate before target shape: S512 is 8.226 ms versus 0.272 ms for dot_k_atomic and 0.416 ms for dense-route dot_k.
decision.s4096_run: skipped because S512 is already decisively negative and the S4096 active-group count is the hard case.
decision.next_step: Keep selected-key MFMA batching and pursue a true segmented/MFMA-friendly streaming reduction or fuse LI/compressor gradients before global dKV writes; do not pursue scalar per-selected local active-list accumulation as the production branch.
